testedConfigurations: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 on PowerVM (VIOS 3.1.4.00) running on IBM Power E1080 (9080-HEX) with Power10 with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 on PowerVM (VIOS 3.1.4.00) running on IBM Power E1080 (9080-HEX) with Power10 without PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE G242-P32-QZ with ARM Ampere(R) Altra(R) Q80-30 with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE G242-P32-QZ with ARM Ampere(R) Altra(R) Q80-30 without PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE R181-Z90-00 with AMD EPYC(TM) 7371 with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE R181-Z90-00 with AMD EPYC(TM) 7371 without PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on IBM z/15 with z15 with PAI SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on IBM z/15 with z15 without PAI SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on Supermicro Super Server SYS-6019P-WTR with Intel(R) Xeon(R) Silver 4215R with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on Supermicro Super Server SYS-6019P-WTR with Intel(R) Xeon(R) Silver 4215R without PAA
certificate.moduleName: SUSE Linux Enterprise GnuTLS Cryptographic Module
certificate.caveat: Interim validation. When operated in approved mode. When installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11 of the Security Policy
certificate.description: GnuTLS is a secure communications library implementing the TLS protocol. It provides a simple C language application programming interface to access the secure communications protocols as well as APIs to parse and write X.509, PKCS#12, and other required structures which is shipped with SUSE Linux Enterprise.

rows: Provides the API for the calling applications to request cryptographic services, and implements the TLS protocol, DRBG, RSA Key Generation, Diffie-Hellman and EC Diffie- Hellman.
Provides the cryptographic algorithm implementations, including AES, SHA, HMAC, RSA Digital Signature and ECDSA.
Provides primitives used by libgnutls and libnettle to support the asymmetric cryptographic operations.
Provides big number arithmetic operations to support the asymmetric cryptographic operations.
The .hmac files contain the HMAC-SHA2-256 values of their associated library for integrity check during the power-up.
